title: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese

seo.title: Cantonese Steamed Fish: Authentic Whole Fish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Cantonese Steamed Fish at home! This easy recipe guides you through making a tender, flavorful whole fish with ginger & scallions. Perfect for a healthy Chinese meal.
longTailKeywords: how to make Cantonese Steamed Fish

best Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese at home
steamed whole fish recipe Chinese style

Hong Kong style steamed fish recipe

Frequently Asked Questions about Steamed Fish
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Cantonese Steamed Fish, a whole fish garnished with ginger, scallions, and cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the delicate flavors of an authentic Cantonese Steamed Fish, a classic Chinese dish that's surprisingly easy to make at home.

description: Cantonese Steamed Fish is a quintessential dish showcasing the delicate flavors of fresh seafood. A whole fish is perfectly steamed with aromatic ginger and scallions, then finished with a sizzling hot oil and savory soy sauce dressing. This simple yet elegant preparation highlights the natural sweetness and tender texture of the fish, making it a beloved centerpiece for any Chinese meal.

ingredients.item: Whole Fish (e.g., Branzino, Tilapia, Red Snapper)
ingredients.notes: cleaned, scaled, gutted, and patted dry
half cut into 2-inch lengths, half julienned for garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Ensure your whole fish is thoroughly cleaned, scaled, and gutted. Pat it completely dry with paper towels. Make two or three shallow diagonal slits on both sides of the fish (this helps it cook evenly). Rub a pinch of white pepper (if using) inside and outside the fish.

instructions.title: Prepare Aromatics and Steamer
instructions.text: Place a few slices of ginger and 2-inch lengths of green onions inside the fish cavity and on top of the fish. Place the remaining 2-inch lengths of green onions on a heatproof plate that fits inside your steamer. This elevates the fish slightly, allowing steam to circulate. Bring a large pot or wok with water to a rolling boil.

instructions.title: Steam the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place the plate with the prepared fish onto a steaming rack inside the pot. Cover tightly and steam for 8-12 minutes, depending on the thickness of your fish. A good indicator is when the flesh turns opaque and flakes easily with a fork. Do not overcook.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce
instructions.text: While the fish is steaming, combine the light soy sauce and sesame oil (if using) in a small bowl. This will be your simple yet flavorful dressing.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the fish is cooked, carefully remove the plate from the steamer. Discard the cooked ginger and green onions that were on and inside the fish, along with any liquid accumulated on the plate (this can taste fishy). Arrange the fresh julienned ginger and green onions, and fresh cilantro sprigs on top of the steamed fish.

instructions.title: Sizzle with Hot Oil
instructions.text: Heat the cooking oil in a small saucepan until it is smoking hot. Carefully pour the hot oil evenly over the julienned ginger, green onions, and cilantro on the fish. You should hear a satisfying sizzle. Immediately pour the prepared soy sauce mixture around the fish, not directly on top to avoid washing away the aromatics. Serve immediately.

tips: Always use a very fresh whole fish for the best results; freshness is key to this dish's delicate flavor. Look for clear eyes, red gills, and firm flesh.
Do not oversteam the fish. It should be just cooked through and still moist. A thicker fish will need slightly longer, while a thinner one will cook faster. Test with a fork at the thickest part.
For an extra layer of flavor, you can add a pinch of sugar to the soy sauce dressing, or a very thin slice of red chili for a mild kick, though traditional Cantonese style is often kept simple.
article: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a beloved Chinese Seafood dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
